tds.note: Healthy mid-range. +3ppm rise reflects bioload accumulation—normal in cycling tank. Still well within safety margin.

reasoning: pH drifting downward despite stable ammonia/nitrite (both 0, ammonia still undetectable suggests cycle not yet fully established or bioload still light). Nitrate holding at 80ppm is good biological signal, but pH decline needs monitoring—could signal substrate detritus buildup or CO2 from organics. Tank is not in distress, but trend bears watching.
recommended_actions: Perform ammonia/nitrite/nitrate test in 24–48 hours to confirm cycle progression
If pH drops below 6.3 in next 12h, do 15% water change with fresh dechlorinated water
Check substrate for visible detritus; light siphon of dead spots if needed
Maintain current feeding schedule—bioload appears controlled
suggested_actuator_actions: None at this time; hold on heater/chiller (temp nominal)
If pH probe recalibrated recently, validate reading with manual test before acting
